item.title: They got duped like a toddler by a grifter.
item.description: “We got duped.” No, you idiot, you gleefully ignored warnings about Project 2025 and made excuses because you’re a cruel asshole who saw all of Trump’s flaws and lifetime of chronic fraud as strength. Those who thought the Deep State was THE enemy were correct. Trump is the enemy. They got duped like a toddler by a grifter. That dissonance between knowing Trump is a teen molester/rapist and ‘moving on’ to project Trump crimes onto gay/trans/drag communities is a humiliation test.
item.dc:creator: IliveINyourHEADrentFREE

item.title: My Catwoman cosplay transition :) - YuzuPyon
item.description: Source: https://www.instagram.com/reel/DMLFwjHRjy_/ Hello everyone! I have been away for a moment, I was studying for a big exam this July, I just took the JLPT N2 which is a pretty advanced Japanese language proficiency test. I wanted to focus on it so I didn't make new content for a while. I heard this audio and I immediately thought of making a Catwoman cosplay finally!!! I have been wanting to cosplay her for years, Michelle Pfeiffer's version might be my favorite but I already had all the materials at home to make her Arkham City's version instead :) I hope you like it! I can also post tutorials on how I made the accessories and make up, if you're interested :) Have a good day all, YuzuPyon
item.dc:creator: yuzupyon

item.title: ADHD work accommodations to avoid anxiety
item.description: Recently my management team is taking an interest in my work after my upper management complimented me for how organized and detailed my work is, saying i am setting the example of how things should be. But now that means my work is being impacted by people who have no idea what my work is just so they can take credit for it to get the same head pats from upper management. There have been a lot of last minute meetings right before submission that are unnecessary in order to be able to say they worked on something with me but it usually is actually them trying to force something into the project last minute that either makes no sense logistically or is out of scope. Last year after feeling overwhelmed by far too many last minute meetings, I went to HR to request work accommodations for my ADHD to not throw last minute meetings on my calendar as well as including an agenda so I can prepare for it in advance and bring the required information instead of scrambling to find things. They accepted this as it is a reasonable request, as well as having a meeting with my managers to discuss this. So for several months these last minute meetings ended. But now we're almost back to the same level. Today, I stood my ground and refused to join, referencing my work accommodations. They still tried to press me, but I didn't let go. If they're interested in my work, get involved before it's done and submitted and not after!
item.dc:creator: PepperoniAndFingernailPizza

item.title: To "spite" an "enemy"
item.description: FP Edit: I don't know about y'all but I'm tired. It's been a rough few days. Trying to find some stress relief learning how to fly an F-18 and a digital simulator. Still don't have all the things like a throttle or stick, but we're getting there. The reason I bring this up, it is important for us to distract ourselves with things that we are interested in any chance we can get. Want to help me out? Follow this https://linktr.ee/GridSquares Between the hiding of the Epstein files, and many other inhumane acts. I bring you the newest gross thing that the Trump Administration has done. Read more here https://www.reuters.com/world/us/us-aid-workers-lobbied-weeks-save-food-stocks-destruction-after-trump-cuts-2025-07-16/ Let me know what you think in the comments below this is an outrageous development amongst many
item.dc:creator: JustGridSquares

item.title: my man knows better than to open the door to uniforms teach your families better
item.description: Also be mindful. I don't work in IT Security, but the folks I know IRL (and whose experience I respect) paint a very very grim future with Palantir. I was born the year Atari started making home systems, well before computers became personal. I always thought Cyberpunk was neat in an abstract fashion, but never wanted to live Shadowrun IRL. Palantir is terrifying in its reach. Be smart.
item.dc:creator: WhattaMattahYou

item.title: Dzień dobry!
item.description:
Woke up to this one trying to eat my toes
So. Much. Energy. But today should be a good day. Despite sleeping on the couch for the first half of the night.
Because this happened.
And you can best believe I wasn't moving. Would you? But, have to take the car into the shop. Whoever designed the 2020 Elantra driver side headlight needs to answer some questions. Like exactly how small are your hands? Because my hand does not fit in that tiny space you designed.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.title: Protip for everyone!
item.description: EDIT: Its Wednesday Frontpage, my Imgurians! Thank you very much! Hope you have a nice week! I do reviews as a hobby, this week I've reviewed the one, the only 'Andor'! Feel free to check it here https://youtu.be/1t_-AL3Fz8s and if you're interested in reading other reviews, I pile them up at https://hypeimmune.wordpress.com/
item.dc:creator: Skullwriter

item.title: GovBrief Today - What Happened #166
item.description: ‼️Trump's executive branch runs on personal loyalty. Today, a man who once called a former U.S. President a "terrorist leader" was rewarded with a top Pentagon job for his allegiance to Trump. At the same time, Trump demanded the prosecution of rival Adam Schiff based on a sudden, baseless accusation of fraud. Earlier, dozens of protesters who showed up at CMU in Pittsburgh where Trump was appearing were pepper-sprayed. This is the new loyalty test in action: dissent is a crime, while allegiance, no matter how extreme your past, is the only thing that matters. They are purging anyone who says no and rewarding anyone who says yes. Don't rely on the algorithm to keep you informed. Let me send this to you every night, free and private, so that you always know what's going on. Add your email at govbrief dot today, on Facebook, or on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com 1. https://go.govbrief.today/appeals-court-wv-mifepristone 2. https://go.govbrief.today/eeoc-transgender-cases-reverse 3. https://go.govbrief.today/pentagon-recalls-half-la-troops 4. https://go.govbrief.today/va-disability-accomodations 5. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-angry-doge-infighting 6. https://go.govbrief.today/senate-confircms-tata 7. https://go.govbrief.today/senate-rescission-vote 8. https://go.govbrief.today/dimon-supports-powell-independence 9. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-accuses-schiff-mortgage 10. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-bondi-epstein-files 11. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-fires-immigration-judges 12. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-redistrict-texas-gerrymander 13. https://go.govbrief.today/house-conservatives-flex-power 14. https://go.govbrief.today/johnson-breaks-trump-epstein 15. https://go.govbrief.today/irs-building-ice-database 16. https://go.govbrief.today/judge-refugees-vetted-allowed 17. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-exiles-5-eswatini 18. https://go.govbrief.today/hhs-formally-fires-thousands 19. https://go.govbrief.today/doj-sues-cpb-board 20. https://go.govbrief.today/nih-coo-fired-contracts Yesterday's Top - https://go.govbrief.today/bondi-english-order Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/pittsburgh-police-pepperspray-protesters Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com I know I've been scarce the last few days, Imgur. Analog life has been intruding. Hope that ends during the next day or so. Y'all take care, be strong, and get out there tomorrow if you can.
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Lily gets a little excited for treats
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: The artist my heart and soul was always longing for...
item.description:
Fellow imgurians, AURORA - If you don't know her art so far, you should check it out! I know, not everybody likes it. Her music has provocative, experimental and dissonant elements. Her subjects are love, peace, suffering, death and spirituality. One of her main topics is also human connectedness with each other and with nature. There are many dark, sad and aggressive songs. For me, mixed with all the other attributes of her art and her personality (as far as I can judge it as I don't know her personally) she's the perfect artist - the one my heart and soul was always longing for...
I just can't get over it, how extraordinarily good her art is – and by that, I mean her overall performance: That incredibly velvety voice, which can also become immensely powerful. Through her physical expression, you get a much deeper sense of how she perceives the music; she lives the music! She lives the things she sings about! She seems to have an intuitive sense and connection for melody and rythm. Her music and lyrics sound honest, serious, but also loving. And I've never heard this perfectly adapted mix of styles that hit your heart to the point. Also the assembly of her band / background singers seems sophisticatedly aligned with the overall concept. And then there’s her style, which feels truly unique.
For me, it started with the Youtube-Video at Tiny Desk Concert (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=evBgLWQwAFA&list=RDevBgLWQwAFA&start_radio=1). I was listening to Billie Eilish and Dua Lipa in a time I felt alone, hurt and sad. Then I saw her performance. My first thought was: "Wow, she's weird... Does she mean it?" But she caught me pretty fast, because I realized, that she deeply felt her art - as much as I felt it.
In her interviews and shorts, she tells the weirdest stuff about, e.g., that she might have peed on stage as she was so nervous and that she collects bugs (there's an interview at the end: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qHPIPdPhUP0&list=RDqHPIPdPhUP0&start_radio=1). In another gig, at the end, she tells the audience, that it's warm up on stage. She smells her armpit and then tells the adience that she smells (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P_XguuxLo10&list=RDXXVwFvkNTEg&index=6). Oh yeah, most of the time, she doesn't wear shoes. In the video linked above you can hear her say: "Live is better without shoes!", while she puts off her shoes... In a very beatiful performance in a church (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XXVwFvkNTEg&list=RDXXVwFvkNTEg&start_radio=1) she starts to tell a story to the audience about her waking up swaeting, forgets about what she wanted to tell, stops the story and goes on with her performance. Everbody in the audience sympathetically laughs... There's more sympathetically weird stuff... But you'll find out yourself, if you're interested.
You’d expect that someone so young, who found success so early, might be seduced by money, power, public feedback – and of course, I have to repeat that I don’t know her personally, but she seems to come along as very humble, reserved, even shy and at the same time funny and full of humor. Even this morning I exchanged some thoughts with a colleague about her. She said, what she likes most about her IS that she's weird. Moreover, she seems pretty reflected but naïvely reflected at the same time. You realize that when she shares thoughts about her life, emotions, life events, personal development, philosophy and the worlds state. And as soon as you experience her art, you'll feel, that she truly seems to have a message.
I know, probably, I sound to you like a fanatic. But I just feel excited about this artist, as I never experienced sth. similar. And I heard a lot of music throughout my life. AURORA is a truly an exceptional artist – the kind we haven’t seen a very long time. She fuses musical genres, spiritual currents, and a sense of naïve, unconditional love into one beautiful, holistic work of art. I've been listening to almost nothing but her music for months now – and I just can't stop it. It never gets boring or old. For me, she hits my heart, my soul and my mind fastly and deeply, like no artist before. Her music helped me through pretty tough times lately... So, I just want to tell you, check it out, if you didn't before! Maybe you'll enjoy as much I do... It’s also worth mentioning that you can’t just LISTEN to AURORA – you really have to SEE her (e.g. via records of her live performances). And in my opinion, she even sounds better live than on record. I’m so looking forward to my first concert of her – and I hope it’s the first of many, many more! Thanks for reading, you imgurian weirdos! [Front page edit] Holy ..... Didn't expect that! Just wanted to share my favorite music artist... Many thanks, you crazy people!!! But of course, AURORA deserves nothing less ;) Corrected some of my language and content as well... Additionally, I was asked for the song titles / links above, so here you go: #1 Starvation, live on KEXP here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=26jzm94Ir6Q&list=RD26jzm94Ir6Q&start_radio=1 #2 The Seed, live Haik concert here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hRhj3lfWzrI&list=RDhRhj3lfWzrI&start_radio=1 #3 Running with the Wolves, Tiny Desk here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kryVBm5YpCg&list=RDkryVBm5YpCg&start_radio=1 AND Runaway, Tiny Desk here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=H9e1TDB4c18&list=RDH9e1TDB4c18&start_radio=1 #4 It was the recorded background song Some Type of Skin, live Vevo here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hSdieDadgM8&list=RDhSdieDadgM8&start_radio=1 #5 AND #6 Through the Eyes of a Child, live at Nidarosdomen here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ncG9zTzsisE&list=RDncG9zTzsisE&start_radio=1 Keep being weired! [Edit 2] Germany, half past 2, have to get up in about 3 1/2 h... I didn't expect front page tonight... And that is not what I enjoyed most... It was really lovely, refreshing, interesting and exciting to chat and share with you'll, my lovely imgurians... Many thanks to you for this nice evening, even if I'll be standing tired in front of my students tomorrow, trying to tell them sth about statistics... Let's see, wish me luck and till next time! Good night!
item.dc:creator: mythm2707
